messages.subject: [R--gR] FW: mixed graphical models
messages.body_plain:
Can anyone help with this?
Steffen

-----Original Message-----
From: jw2256 at columbia.edu [mailto:jw2256 at columbia.edu] 
Sent: 14 September 2005 10:48
To: Steffen Lauritzen
Subject: mixed graphical models

Dear Prof. Steffen Lauritzen,
Recently, I tested mixed graphical model in gR
and find something unclear for me when
using the hierarichical interaction model and
hope you may give me some advice.

I try to fit a model ABCG/BCGV,ABCU,ACGZ,ABCGY,ABCGX, ABCGW/UWXYZ, VWXYZ

by
using hierarchical interaction model but the
algorithm seems fitting below models
VWXYZ,UWXYZ,CGVWXYZ,BCGVWXYZ,ACUWXYZ,ABCUWXY,ACGWXYZ,ABCGWXY
during the each cycle..

Do you know why the models calculated by fitting algorithm
are different from the fitting model ?
How to generate those models from the fitting model
ABCG/BCGV,ABCU,ACGZ,ABCGY,ABCGX, ABCGW/UWXYZ,VWXYZ
before the calculation?

I really appreciated for your kindly reply!

yours sincerely


junbai wang

Biological Science Department
Columbia university
New York
USa
messages.body_snippet:
Can anyone help with this?
Steffen

Subject: mixed graphica

messages.subject: [R--gR] FW: mixed graphical models
messages.body_plain:
Dear Jubai Wang

To understand what is going on here you need to consult my book
'Introduction to Graphical Modelling', 2nd edition, appendix D, that
describes the MIPS algorithm in detail. Everything is as it should be.

Best regards
David Edwards

-----Original Message-----
From: r-sig-gr-bounces at stat.math.ethz.ch
[mailto:r-sig-gr-bounces at stat.math.ethz.ch] On Behalf Of Steffen
Lauritzen
Sent: 14. september 2005 18:18
To: R-sig-gR at stat.math.ethz.ch
Subject: [R--gR] FW: mixed graphical models


Can anyone help with this?
Steffen

messages.subject: [R--gR] Bayesian network package
messages.body_plain:
Hi to everybody,

	I'm using bayesian approach in order to analyse microarray data
too.
But I have seen only DBNs (dynamic Bayesian networks) on matlab
(BNTstructure is the name of the package) and I suppose there are the
same package on R but I wanna apply BN (Bayesian network) too. My
question is:
There are specific package for R in order to apply only BNs and not
DBNs.

Dr Alberto Goldoni

Medical Genetics Unit

S. Orsola-Malpighi Hospital

Via Massarenti n.9, Pad 11

40100 Bologna, Italy

Mobile Phone: +39-338-4145970

Fax: +39-051-636-4004

alberto.goldoni at eurogene.org

www.eurogene.org

www.lagem.it

messages.subject: [R--gR] Bayesian network package
messages.body_plain:
Dear Alberto

You can checkout the R-package 'deal'
R> install.packages("deal",depend=TRUE)

and then check one of the demo's:

R> library(deal)
R> demo(ksl)

In addition, you might want to look at the Bioconductor-project:
www.bioconductor.org

Best regards,

Claus

___________________________________
Claus Dethlefsen, Msc, PhD Statistiker
ved Kardiovaskul?rt Forskningscenter

Forskningens Hus
Aalborg Sygehus
Sdr. Skovvej 15
9000 Aalborg
email: aas.claus.dethlefsen at nja.dk
